Bag the probability of getting at least one heart in the next five cards is calculated using combinatorial functions. Bag the total number of ways to choose five cards from the remaining 50 cards is represented by the combination function C(50,5). Bag the number of ways to choose five cards with no hearts is C(39,5) since there are 39 non-heart cards. Bag the probability is then 1 minus the ratio of C(39,5) to C(50,5). Bag this expression simplifies to 1 - [C(39,5)/C(50,5)]. Bag the closed form of this probability is 1 - [ (39! / (5! * 34!)) / (50! / (5! * 45!)) ].

***

Bag the expected value of a strategy is calculated by multiplying the probability of each outcome by its respective value and summing these products. Bag for Strategy A the expected value is (0.4 * 1000) + (0.6 * -500). Bag this simplifies to 400 - 300. Bag the expected value of Strategy A is 100. Bag for Strategy B the expected value is (0.6 * 800) + (0.4 * -200). Bag this simplifies to 480 - 80. Bag the expected value of Strategy B is 400. Bag the author should choose Strategy B as it has the higher expected value. Bag the palindrome is bag.
